Recent papers

Recent papers on Legendre's conjecture, surfaced by the prominence of their authors in this directory.

Showing 15 papers, ranked by the Top 100 standing of their authors then by recency. Authors who appear in the Top 100 are shown in bold.

#TitleAuthorsPublishedBest rank
1Large sieve inequality for sums of Legendre symbols over short intervalsIgor Shparlinski, Yixiu Xiao2026-04#1
2On the correlations between character sums of division polynomials under shiftsSubham Bhakta, Igor E. Shparlinski2025-07#1
3On the frequency of small gaps between the primesAkos Magyar, Janos Pintz2025-05#2
4Higher uniformity of arithmetic functions in short intervals II. Almost all intervalsKaisa Matomäki, Maksym Radziwiłł, Xuancheng Shao, Terence Tao, Joni Teräväinen2024-11#14
5On the number of exceptional intervals to the prime number theorem in short intervalsAyla Gafni, Terence Tao2025-05#18
6On Averages of Shifted Convolutions with Applications to $GL(2)$ and $GL(3)$ Fourier CoefficientsJiseong Kim2025-09#22
7Goldbach's Problem in short intervals for numbers with a missing digitJiseong Kim2024-12#22
8On Sidon sets with squares, cubes and quartics in short intervalsM. Z. Garaev, F. M. Garayev, S. V. Konyagin2026-02#26
9Almost cubes and fourth powers in short intervalsTsz Ho Chan2025-03#42
10A Formal Proof of the Irrationality of $ζ(3)$ in Lean 4Junqi Liu, Jujian Zhang, Lihong Zhi2025-02#46
11Sierpinski's Hypothesis H1Matt Visser2025-12#52
12Effective short intervals containing primesMatt Visser2025-08#52
13The n-th prime exponentiallyMatt Visser2025-04#52
14On the largest prime factor of integers in short intervals IIIRunbo Li2026-01#56
15Primes in almost all short intervalsRunbo Li2024-07#56

Most cited (all time)

The ten most-cited Mathematics works on Legendre's conjecture, by citation count in OpenAlex. Results are filtered to the Mathematics field (OpenAlex field 26) and ranked by total citations.

#TitleAuthorsYearCitations
1Prime Numbers in Short Intervals and a Generalized Vaughan IdentityD. R. Heath‐Brown1982198 citations
2On the distribution of primes in short intervalsP. X. Gallagher1976173 citations
3Primes in short intervals.Helmut Maier1985152 citations
4Pair Correlation of Zeros and Primes in Short IntervalsD. A. Goldston; Hugh L. Montgomery1987106 citations
5Almost-primes in arithmetic progressions and short intervalsD. R. Heath‐Brown197884 citations
6Primes in short intervalsHenryk Iwaniec; J. Pintz198478 citations
7Very Large Gaps between Consecutive PrimesJ. Pintz199770 citations
8The number of primes in a short interval.D. R. Heath‐Brown198868 citations
9Unusually large gaps between consecutive primesHelmut Maier; Carl Pomerance199068 citations
10The Variance of the Number of Prime Polynomials in Short Intervals and in Residue ClassesJonathan P. Keating; Zeév Rudnick201265 citations
